headline: In Virginia, the world's data center capital faces mounting opposition from local residents
description: This East Coast US state is home to 12% of the world's capacity for the massive data centers essential to AI, which it has attracted largely through tax breaks. Local residents are especially concerned about their environmental impact.

headline: 39 in custody in Paris over banned far-right and anti-fascist marches
In Hungary, jubilation marks Péter Magyar's inauguration as prime minister
Spanish island braces for arrival of Hantavirus cruise ship
description: The MV Hondius, hit by a hantavirus outbreak, is set to anchor off Tenerife, in the Canary Islands, on Sunday. About 80 passengers will be evacuated by Zodiac boats and immediately repatriated by plane. The operation is being overseen by the World Health Organization's director general.

headline: Mali: Dozens killed in fresh attacks, say local and security sources
description: One official said the latest attacks by armed factions like the Al-Qaeda-linked Group for the Support of Islam and Muslims had brought the death toll to over 70 in recent days.

headline: Is it really possible to tax oil companies' windfall profits?
description: As fuel prices soar, the idea of taxing multinational oil companies' windfall profits has resurfaced. Yet there's heated debate over whether such a tax is actually possible.

headline: A race to photograph vanishing cinemas
description: A Swiss cinematographer and filmmaker has traveled the world to document old cinemas and preserve the memory of endangered heritage and striking architecture. Simon Edelstein's latest book focuses on India.

headline: Israeli strikes in Lebanon kill at least 8 despite ceasefire
Is it ever a good idea to... coordinate outfits (when you're a couple)?
Russia celebrates Victory Day with lackluster parade
UK sends warship to Middle East
French Socialists fracture over presidential primary
US economy persists through trade uncertainty
First French prototypes that extract CO₂ from the air offer promise and uncertainty
Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas's maneuvers to  lock in the Fatah congress
Bulgaria's new PM unveils roadmap, balancing pro-European and pro-Russian interests
Military deterrence has fully entered 'third nuclear age'
Musk lashes out at French judges investigating X
Annette Messager's exhibition brings her menagerie to life at the Musée de la Chasse et de la Nature
Pedro Almodóvar still burns with desire for cinema
description: Born during Madrid's cultural awakening, his work is celebrated around the world. But, despite several appearances at the Cannes Film Festival, Pedro Almodóvar has never won the Palme d'Or. Now 76 years old, the pioneer of Spanish auteur cinema is back in competition with 'Bitter Christmas,' in which he delves into the labyrinth of creation.

headline: Six star Japanese products for umami recipes
description: These emblematic ingredients of Japanese cuisine are on the menu in these six French-Japanese recipes, accompanied by tips on how to use them easily on a daily basis.

headline: How can I create a chemical-free nest for my child?
description: Ubiquitous in our daily lives, chemical substances prove particularly toxic for young children, starting from pregnancy. Environmental health workshops educate parents on these sources of pollution, and offer solutions to protect their offspring.

headline: The UK is threatened by the far right
'Germany lacks breakthrough innovation'
'Trump is now portrayed by some of his opponents as the Antichrist'
